title: Adenochloa adenophora
taxonomy: Adenochloa adenophora belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a type of grass that is native to parts of Asia, particularly in the Himalayan region. The plant's classification also includes the genus Adenochloa, which is a monotypic genus containing a single species. Adenochloa adenophora is a perennial grass that grows in a variety of habitats, including wetlands and rocky areas. This plant is also known as Himalayan feather grass due to its distinctive feathery plumes.

parts: Adenochloa adenophora, also known as the Chinese silvergrass or needle grass, is used in traditional Chinese medicine. The medicinal parts of this plant include the roots and rhizomes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
